rationale: Approval holds at 8b945359, and the NEW R11 prerequisite (rebase/merge-forward spec/108 onto post-107 main before plan approve) is executable under the project's git rules on every axis. (1) Pre-commit hook (dispatch.go) blocks spec/* commits ONLY in implement mode (:122); the R11 op runs in plan mode (post-spec-approve, pre-plan-approve) -> not blocked. Moreover git rebase fires post-rewrite and git merge fires pre-merge-commit, so the pre-commit hook is never even invoked; main is protected any-mode but the op targets spec/108, not main. (2) No lifecycle SHA pin: the epic<->spec link resolves by spec ID via beads metadata (FindEpicBySpecID -> SpecIDFromMetadata from num+title, cache.go:141-175), and phase/mode lives in the epic's mindspec_phase metadata (ADR-0023) — both survive a SHA-rewriting rebase. grep found zero ancestry/base-commit/descendant guards in approve/phase/spec/guard, so nothing asserts spec/108 must descend from a fixed base. (3) Gate-forward-BENEFICIAL for my axis: impl-approve measures merge-base(main,specBranch)..specBranch (approve/impl.go:241); post-rebase the merge-base is post-107 main so 108's cumulative diff correctly excludes 107's merged work, and per-bead complete uses merge-base(specBranch,beadHead) (complete.go:399), fork points unaffected. R11 names an adequate procedure (rebase OR merge-forward). Reinstated R3 findRoot removal is consistent with R11 (bead verifies findRoot absent post-rebase; AC line 96 all-three-absent). My sole round-1 finding stays non-blocking.
findings: R11 EXECUTABILITY (the round-2 focus): SOUND. dispatch.go:122 gates spec/* commits to implement mode only; the R11 rebase/merge-forward occurs in plan mode, so it is not blocked. Independently, git rebase invokes post-rewrite (not pre-commit) and git merge invokes pre-merge-commit (not pre-commit) — dispatch.go's runPreCommit is not triggered by either operation regardless of mode. The escape hatch is not required. No lifecycle blocker exists, so the spec's stated procedure ('rebased or merged-forward onto post-107 main before plan approve', R11) is complete and correct.

NO SHA PIN ANYWHERE IN THE LIFECYCLE. The epic->spec association is resolved purely by spec ID (phase/cache.go:141-175 FindEpicBySpecID maps specID via SpecIDFromMetadata over AllEpics; derive.go:525-532). plan approve re-derives the epic through FindEpicBySpecID at call time (approve/plan.go:113) — a rebase changes commit SHAs but not the spec ID, num, or title, so the link is preserved. Phase/mode is beads metadata (mindspec_phase), not commit-keyed. grep for is-ancestor/descendant/base-commit/fork-point guards across internal/{approve,phase,spec,guard} returned nothing: no gate rejects a rebased-history spec branch. The panel BRIEF's recorded commit SHAs are review bookkeeping, not a lifecycle constraint.

GATE-FORWARDNESS POSITIVELY IMPROVED by the rebase. The impl-approve doc-sync/divergence gate anchors on MergeBase('main', specBranch) (approve/impl.go:241,274). After rebasing/merge-forwarding onto post-107 main, that merge-base is post-107 main, so the cumulative gate diff sees ONLY 108's own changes and excludes 107's already-merged edits to internal/validate/plan.go — exactly the forward behavior R11 intends. The per-bead complete gate uses MergeBase(specBranch, beadHead) (complete.go:399), whose fork points are unaffected since beads fork after the rebase. Both merge-forward and rebase yield the same merge-base outcome; the spec correctly offers either.

R3 findRoot reinstatement (round-2 change B) is internally consistent with R11. R3 now removes all three unparam carve-outs with the findRoot removal gated on the R11 rebase (post-rebase findRoot is deleted by 107, so the exclusion matches nothing and its removal is inert); the implementing bead MUST verify internal/next/beads.go contains no findRoot before deleting (spec line 44). AC line 96 updated to all-three-absent, AC line 97 golangci clean 'evaluated at implementation time, i.e. post-R11 rebase'. Ordering and evidence (probe was --no-config, bypassing all exclusions) are precise and correct.

NON-BLOCKING (carried from round 1, unchanged): R1 (spec line 40) still calls .mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml 'workflow-owned non-doc source' requiring a same-diff workflow doc touch. Per docsync.go isDocFile (matches .mindspec/domains/ via lifecycleDocSubtrees), it is DOC-classified and isProcessArtifact skips it before attribution, and it self-satisfies as the workflow companion (hasArtifactPrefix domains/workflow). The baked-in constraint is over-conservative, hence forward-SAFE (extra doc touches never fail a gate). Wording fix, not a gate blocker; approval unaffected.
